tool_args.label: Corporate Governance Committee Meeting - May 28, 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Corporate Governance Committee Meeting - Wednesday, 5/28/2025, 8:30 - 9:00 AM ET, 2 BROADWAY 20TH FLOOR BOARD ROOM

payload.description: MTA Corporate Governance Committee Meeting at 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room, 8:30-9:00 AM ET. Agenda items include public comments, approval of minutes, review and approve procurement guidelines, review whistleblower policy, review codes of ethics, review and approve policies in connection with Public Authorities Law.

tool_args.label: Corporate Governance Committee Meeting - December 16, 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Chairman Lieber called the December 16, 2024 meeting of the MTA Corporate Governance Committee to order at 11:55 AM. Chair Lieber adjourned December 16, 2024 meeting of the Corporate Governance Committee at 12:00 PM.

payload.description: Prior Corporate Governance Committee meeting. Called to order at 11:55 AM, adjourned at 12:00 PM. Agenda included public comments, approval of minutes, 2025 Draft Committee Work Plan, Review of MTA By-Laws, Review and Approve Board Committee Charters, Review of Governance Guidelines, Review and Approve MTA Policies in Connection with Provisions of Public Authorities Law.

tool_args.label: SBDP Tier 1 threshold increase
tool_args.raw_text: Section 1265-b was amended to increase the threshold for Tier 1 SBDP contracts from $1 million to $1.5 million

payload.claim_text: Public Authorities Law, Section 1265-b was amended to increase the threshold for Tier 1 SBDP contracts from $1 million to $1.5 million.
payload.description: Threshold increase for Tier 1 Small Business Development Program contracts
payload.subject: Tier 1 SBDP contracts

tool_args.label: SBDP Tier 2 threshold increase
tool_args.raw_text: Section 1265-b was amended to increase the threshold for Tier 2 SBDP contracts from $3 million to $5 million

payload.description: Threshold increase for Tier 2 Small Business Development Program contracts

tool_args.label: All Agency Service Contract Procurement Guidelines
tool_args.raw_text: ALL AGENCY SERVICE CONTRACT PROCUREMENT GUIDELINES - Adopted by the Board on March-May 287, 20254. These guidelines apply to the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), the New York City Transit Authority (NYCT), the Long Island Rail Road Company (LIRR), The Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company (MNR), the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority (SIRTOA), the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority (MaBSTOA), MTA Construction and Development (MTAC&D), MTA Bus Company (MTA Bus), First Mutual Transportation Assurance Co. (FMTAC), Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company (GCMC), and the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (Bridges and Tunnels)

payload.project_name: All Agency Service Contract Procurement Guidelines
payload.description: Service Contract Procurement Guidelines for the MTA, NYCT, LIRR, MNR, SIRTOA, MaBSTOA, MTAC&D, MTA Bus, FMTAC, GCMC, and Bridges and Tunnels. Covers personal service contracts and miscellaneous service contracts.

tool_args.label: All Agency General Contract Procurement Guidelines
tool_args.raw_text: ALL AGENCY GENERAL CONTRACT PROCUREMENT GUIDELINES - Adopted by the Board on March 27, 2024 May 28, 2025. These guidelines apply to the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), the New York City Transit Authority (NYCT), the Long Island Rail Road Company (LIRR), The Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company (MNR), the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority (SIRTOA), the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority (MaBSTOA), MTA Construction and Development (MTAC&D), MTA Bus Company (MTA Bus), First Mutual Transportation Assurance Co. (FMTAC), Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company (GCMC), and the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (Bridges and Tunnels)

payload.project_name: All Agency General Contract Procurement Guidelines
payload.description: General Contract Procurement Guidelines applying to Purchase Contracts, Public Work Contracts, and Miscellaneous Procurement Contracts for the MTA and its agencies.

tool_args.label: MTA Whistleblower Protection Policy
tool_args.raw_text: WHISTLEBLOWER PROTECTION - Policy Number 11-041, Responsible Agency/Department MTAHQ/General Counsel, Effective Date October 20, 2021

payload.description: All Agency Policy Directive No. 11-041 - Whistleblower Protection. To encourage and enable the reporting by MTA employees of wrongful acts, protect MTA employees from retaliation for whistleblowing and ensure that the MTA complies with applicable law protecting whistleblowers.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: All-Agency Code of Ethics
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Code of Ethics Adopted by the MTA Board. Originally Adopted May 25, 2005. Revised March 29, 2006, March 28, 2007, December 16, 2009, November 16, 2011, February 26, 2014, November 19, 2014, December 16, 2015, March 29, 2023.

payload.description: MTA All-Agency Code of Ethics. Originally adopted May 25, 2005 and revised multiple times including March 29, 2023. Covers gifts, conflicts of interest, financial disclosure, future employment restrictions, discipline, and more.

tool_args.label: Board Members Code of Ethics
tool_args.raw_text: Board Members Code of Ethics. Originally Issued March 2006. Revised March 2007, December 2009, November 2011, November 2014, March 2023.

payload.description: MTA Board Members Code of Ethics. Originally issued March 2006, revised through March 2023. Covers confidentiality, gifts, conflicts of interest, political activities, post-service restrictions.

tool_args.label: Small Business Development Program
tool_args.raw_text: Small Business Development Program (\"SBDP\") contracts - Section 1265-b was amended to increase the threshold for Tier 1 SBDP contracts from $1 million to $1.5 million and Tier 2 SBDP contracts from $3 million to $5 million.

payload.description: Program under Public Authorities Law Section 1265-b. Tier 1 contracts up to $1.5 million, Tier 2 contracts up to $5 million.
